revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Summer festivals in Japan]][[ja:夏祭り]][[zh:夏祭]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Summer festivals in Japan]][[ja:夏祭り]][[zh:夏祭]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Summer festivals in Japan]][[ja:夏祭り]][[zh:夏祭]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11430090]] Wikidata name: Summer festivals in Japan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses}} {{Unreferenced|date=August 2014}} {{nihongo|Natsumatsuri|夏祭り|1=Natsu Matsuri}} refers to the general term for {{ill|summer festivals|ja|夏祭り|1=Summer Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/summer festivals|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsu Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430090}} held in [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. These festivals are typically held from early July to late August, although festivals held in September that correspond to the {{ill|lunisolar calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lunisolar calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}'s {{ill|July (Lunisolar)|ja|7月 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/qq|lt=seventh month|qq=}} are often treated as summer festivals<ref>Examples include Kimata Town Fireworks Festival and Arima Town Bon Dance.</ref>.
== Overview == Many of Japan's summer festivals are rooted in events such as {{ill|Obon|ja|盂蘭盆会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obon|1=Ullambana Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15898039}} (盂蘭盆会), [[:en:Qixi Festival|Tanabata]], and [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Matsuri]], or related rituals. As such, these festivals align with the sixth and seventh months of the old lunisolar calendar. In rural areas, these events often originated as part of relieving labor fatigue from summer farming activities. In urban areas, they are believed to stem from pre-[[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] traditions of warding off summer epidemics and honoring the dead.
However, modernization has caused significant transformations in these festivals, and their origins are often not recognized. Summer festivals are now largely regarded as vibrant and festive events rather than solemn ones. Furthermore, many festivals lack origins before the Edo period, having been introduced in the modern era or modeled after traditional festivals from other regions.
Especially around the {{ill|Obon|ja|盂蘭盆会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obon|1=Ullambana Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15898039}} season in August, summer festivals are often associated with homecoming, creating opportunities for emotional reunions with loved ones{{Citation needed|date=October 2014}}.
In port towns, variations rich in local flavor, such as "Minato Matsuri" (harbor festivals), are common. Many [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] festivals also take place during this period.
== Events Held at Summer Festivals == * {{ill|Obon#Bon_Odori|lt=Bon Dance|ja|盆踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obon#Bon_Odori|1=Bon Odori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1152364}} * [[:en:Parade|Parades]] * {{ill|Fireworks|ja|花火大会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fireworks|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 花火|1=firework|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q127933}} * [[:en:Market stall|Yatai]] (food stalls, often at [[:en:Ennichi|Ennichi]]) * [[:en:Matsuri float|Floats]] and {{ill|Festival music|ja|祭囃子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival music|qq=}} * Events using temporary stages: ** Song shows ** Character shows (often featuring {{ill|Anime|ja|アニメ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anime|1=Anime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4066283}}, {{ill|Super Sentai|ja|戦隊シリーズ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Super Sentai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スーパー戦隊シリーズ|1=Super Sentai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1054760}}, or [[:en:Local hero (Japan)|Local hero (Japan)]]) ** [[:en:Magic (illusion)|Magic (illusion)]] shows ** [[:en:Karaoke|Karaoke]] competitions
== Representative Summer Festivals of Japan == [[File:Aomori Nebuta Festival Float August 2006.jpg|thumb|180px|{{ill|Aomori Nebuta Festival|ja|青森ねぶた祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomori Nebuta Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 青森ねぶた|1=Aomori Nebuta Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q862407}}]] [[File:Sendai Tanabata Festival 2010.jpg|thumb|180px|{{ill|Sendai Tanabata Festival|ja|仙台七夕まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai Tanabata Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仙台七夕}}]] [[File:Tomioka hachimangu10.jpg|thumb|180px|[[:en:Fukagawa Matsuri|Fukagawa Matsuri]]]] [[File:Gion Matsuri 2017-5.jpg|thumb|180px|[[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Matsuri]]]] [[File:Awa-odori 2008 Tokushima.jpg|thumb|180px|[[:en:Awa Dance Festival|Awa Odori]]]] * Festival|ja|函館港まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakodate Port Festival|qq=}} – Features dances like the {{ill|Squid Dance|ja|いか踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Squid Dance|qq=}}. * [[:en:Aomori Tohoku|ja|東北三大祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Festivals of Tohoku|qq=}}. ** {{ill|Hirosaki Neputa Matsuri|lt=Hirosaki Neputa Matsuri|lt=Hirosaki Neputa Prefecture]] ** {{ill|Akita Kantō|ja|秋田竿燈まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanto Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 竿燈}} – One of the Three Great Festivals of Tohoku. * [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] ** {{ill|Iida-machi Toro-yama Festival|ja|飯田町燈籠山祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iida-machi Toro-yama Festival|qq=}} * [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]] ** Prefecture]] ** [[:en:Tenno Matsuri|Owari Tsushima Tennō Festival]] – One of the {{ill|Three Great River Festivals of Japan|ja|日本三大川祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great River Festivals of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]] ** [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Matsuri]] – One of the {{ill|Three Great Festivals of Japan|ja|日本三大祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Festivals of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本三大一覧|1=Japan's Top 3|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10311536}}. * [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] ** [[:en:Tenjin Matsuri|Tenjin Matsuri]] – Part of the Three Great Festivals of Japan and the {{ill|Three Great Summer Festivals of Osaka|ja|大阪三大夏祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Great Summer Festivals of Osaka|qq=}}. * [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]] ** {{ill|Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|lt=Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|lt=Yumura <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Summer festivals in Japan}} [[zh:夏祭]] <!--zhwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Summer festivals in Japan}} [[zh:夏祭]] {{wikidata link|Q11430090}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Culture of Japan]] [[Category:Festivals]] [[Category:Festivals in Japan]] [[Category:Seasonal terms for summer]] [[Category:Summer]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Summer festivals in Japan]][[ja:夏祭り]][[zh:夏祭]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Summer festivals in Japan]][[ja:夏祭り]][[zh:夏祭]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Summer festivals in Japan]][[ja:夏祭り]][[zh:夏祭]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11430090]] Wikidata name: Summer festivals in Japan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses}} {{Unreferenced|date=August 2014}} {{nihongo|Natsumatsuri|夏祭り|1=Natsu Matsuri}} refers to the general term for {{ill|summer festivals|ja|夏祭り|1=Summer Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/summer festivals|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsu Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430090}} held in [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. These festivals are typically held from early July to late August, although festivals held in September that correspond to the {{ill|lunisolar calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lunisolar calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}'s {{ill|July (Lunisolar)|ja|7月 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/qq|lt=seventh month|qq=}} are often treated as summer festivals<ref>Examples include Kimata Town Fireworks Festival and Arima Town Bon Dance.</ref>.
== Overview == Many of Japan's summer festivals are rooted in events such as {{ill|Obon|ja|盂蘭盆会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obon|1=Ullambana Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15898039}} (盂蘭盆会), [[:en:Qixi Festival|Tanabata]], and [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Matsuri]], or related rituals. As such, these festivals align with the sixth and seventh months of the old lunisolar calendar. In rural areas, these events often originated as part of relieving labor fatigue from summer farming activities. In urban areas, they are believed to stem from pre-[[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] traditions of warding off summer epidemics and honoring the dead.
However, modernization has caused significant transformations in these festivals, and their origins are often not recognized. Summer festivals are now largely regarded as vibrant and festive events rather than solemn ones. Furthermore, many festivals lack origins before the Edo period, having been introduced in the modern era or modeled after traditional festivals from other regions.
Especially around the {{ill|Obon|ja|盂蘭盆会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obon|1=Ullambana Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15898039}} season in August, summer festivals are often associated with homecoming, creating opportunities for emotional reunions with loved ones{{Citation needed|date=October 2014}}.
In port towns, variations rich in local flavor, such as "Minato Matsuri" (harbor festivals), are common. Many [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] festivals also take place during this period.

[[zh:%E5%A4%8F%E7%A5%AD]] [[ja:夏祭り]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Summer festivals in Japan}} [[zh:夏祭]] <!--zhwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Summer festivals in Japan}} [[zh:夏祭]] {{wikidata link|Q11430090}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Culture of Japan]] [[Category:Festivals]] [[Category:Festivals in Japan]] [[Category:Seasonal terms for summer]] [[Category:Summer]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Summer festivals in Japan]][[ja:夏祭り]][[zh:夏祭]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Summer festivals in Japan]][[ja:夏祭り]][[zh:夏祭]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Summer festivals in Japan]][[ja:夏祭り]][[zh:夏祭]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11430090]] Wikidata name: Summer festivals in Japan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses}} {{Unreferenced|date=August 2014}} {{nihongo|Natsumatsuri|夏祭り|1=Natsu Matsuri}} refers to the general term for {{ill|summer festivals|ja|夏祭り|1=Summer Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/summer festivals|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsu Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430090}} held in [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. These festivals are typically held from early July to late August, although festivals held in September that correspond to the {{ill|lunisolar calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lunisolar calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}'s {{ill|July (Lunisolar)|ja|7月 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/qq|lt=seventh month|qq=}} are often treated as summer festivals<ref>Examples include Kimata Town Fireworks Festival and Arima Town Bon Dance.</ref>.
== Overview == Many of Japan's summer festivals are rooted in events such as {{ill|Obon|ja|盂蘭盆会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obon|1=Ullambana Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15898039}} (盂蘭盆会), [[:en:Qixi Festival|Tanabata]], and [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Matsuri]], or related rituals. As such, these festivals align with the sixth and seventh months of the old lunisolar calendar. In rural areas, these events often originated as part of relieving labor fatigue from summer farming activities. In urban areas, they are believed to stem from pre-[[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] traditions of warding off summer epidemics and honoring the dead.
However, modernization has caused significant transformations in these festivals, and their origins are often not recognized. Summer festivals are now largely regarded as vibrant and festive events rather than solemn ones. Furthermore, many festivals lack origins before the Edo period, having been introduced in the modern era or modeled after traditional festivals from other regions.
Especially around the {{ill|Obon|ja|盂蘭盆会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obon|1=Ullambana Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15898039}} season in August, summer festivals are often associated with homecoming, creating opportunities for emotional reunions with loved ones{{Citation needed|date=October 2014}}.
In port towns, variations rich in local flavor, such as "Minato Matsuri" (harbor festivals), are common. Many [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] festivals also take place during this period.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Summer festivals in Japan]][[ja:夏祭り]][[zh:夏祭]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Summer festivals in Japan]][[ja:夏祭り]][[zh:夏祭]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Summer festivals in Japan]][[ja:夏祭り]][[zh:夏祭]]
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11430090]]
Wikidata name: Summer festivals in Japan
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Otheruses}}
{{Unreferenced|date=August 2014}}
{{nihongo|Natsumatsuri|夏祭り|1=Natsu Matsuri}} refers to the general term for {{ill|summer festivals|ja|夏祭り|1=Summer Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/summer festivals|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsu Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430090}} held in {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. These festivals are typically held from early July to late August, although festivals held in September that correspond to the {{ill|lunisolar calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lunisolar calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}'s {{ill|July (Lunisolar)|ja|7月 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/qq|lt=seventh month|qq=}} are often treated as summer festivals<ref>Examples include Kimata Town Fireworks Festival and Arima Town Bon Dance.</ref>.

== Overview ==
Many of Japan's summer festivals are rooted in events such as {{ill|Obon|ja|盂蘭盆会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obon|1=Ullambana Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15898039}} (盂蘭盆会), {{ill|Qixi Festival|en|Qixi Festival|WD=Q13423122|lt=Tanabata}}, and {{ill|Gion Matsuri|en|Gion Matsuri|WD=Q979873|lt=Gion Matsuri}}, or related rituals. As such, these festivals align with the sixth and seventh months of the old lunisolar calendar. In rural areas, these events often originated as part of relieving labor fatigue from summer farming activities. In urban areas, they are believed to stem from pre-{{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}} traditions of warding off summer epidemics and honoring the dead.

However, modernization has caused significant transformations in these festivals, and their origins are often not recognized. Summer festivals are now largely regarded as vibrant and festive events rather than solemn ones. Furthermore, many festivals lack origins before the Edo period, having been introduced in the modern era or modeled after traditional festivals from other regions.

Especially around the {{ill|Obon|ja|盂蘭盆会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obon|1=Ullambana Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15898039}} season in August, summer festivals are often associated with homecoming, creating opportunities for emotional reunions with loved ones{{Citation needed|date=October 2014}}.

In port towns, variations rich in local flavor, such as "Minato Matsuri" (harbor festivals), are common. Many {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} festivals also take place during this period.

== Events Held at Summer Festivals ==
* {{ill|Obon#Bon_Odori|lt=Bon Dance|ja|盆踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obon#Bon_Odori|1=Bon Odori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1152364}}
* {{ill|Parade|en|Parade|WD=Q657449|lt=Parades}}
* {{ill|Fireworks|ja|花火大会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fireworks|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 花火|1=firework|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q127933}}
* {{ill|Market stall|en|Market stall|WD=Q16556730|lt=Yatai}} (food stalls, often at {{ill|Ennichi|en|Ennichi|WD=Q985655|lt=Ennichi}})
* {{ill|Matsuri float|en|Matsuri float|WD=Q11470974|lt=Floats}} and {{ill|Festival music|ja|祭囃子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival music|qq=}}
* Events using temporary stages:
** Song shows
** Character shows (often featuring {{ill|Anime|ja|アニメ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anime|1=Anime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4066283}}, {{ill|Super Sentai|ja|戦隊シリーズ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Super Sentai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スーパー戦隊シリーズ|1=Super Sentai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1054760}}, or {{ill|Local hero (Japan)|en|Local hero (Japan)|WD=Q4259512|lt=Local hero (Japan)}})
** {{ill|Magic (illusion)|en|Magic (illusion)|WD=Q148442|lt=Magic (illusion)}} shows
** {{ill|Karaoke|en|Karaoke|WD=Q229345|lt=Karaoke}} competitions

{{Otheruses}}
{{Unreferenced|date=August 2014}}
{{nihongo|Natsumatsuri|夏祭り|1=Natsu Matsuri}} refers to the general term for {{ill|summer festivals|ja|夏祭り|1=Summer Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/summer festivals|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsu Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430090}} held in {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. These festivals are typically held from early July to late August, although festivals held in September that correspond to the {{ill|lunisolar calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lunisolar calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}'s {{ill|July (Lunisolar)|ja|7月 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/qq|lt=seventh month|qq=}} are often treated as summer festivals<ref>Examples include Kimata Town Fireworks Festival and Arima Town Bon Dance.</ref>.

== Overview ==
Many of Japan's summer festivals are rooted in events such as {{ill|Obon|ja|盂蘭盆会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obon|1=Ullambana Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15898039}} (盂蘭盆会), {{ill|Qixi Festival|en|Qixi Festival|WD=Q13423122|lt=Tanabata}}, and {{ill|Gion Matsuri|en|Gion Matsuri|WD=Q979873|lt=Gion Matsuri}}, or related rituals. As such, these festivals align with the sixth and seventh months of the old lunisolar calendar. In rural areas, these events often originated as part of relieving labor fatigue from summer farming activities. In urban areas, they are believed to stem from pre-{{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}} traditions of warding off summer epidemics and honoring the dead.

However, modernization has caused significant transformations in these festivals, and their origins are often not recognized. Summer festivals are now largely regarded as vibrant and festive events rather than solemn ones. Furthermore, many festivals lack origins before the Edo period, having been introduced in the modern era or modeled after traditional festivals from other regions.

Especially around the {{ill|Obon|ja|盂蘭盆会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obon|1=Ullambana Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15898039}} season in August, summer festivals are often associated with homecoming, creating opportunities for emotional reunions with loved ones{{Citation needed|date=October 2014}}.

In port towns, variations rich in local flavor, such as "Minato Matsuri" (harbor festivals), are common. Many {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} festivals also take place during this period.

== Events Held at Summer Festivals ==
* {{ill|Obon#Bon_Odori|lt=Bon Dance|ja|盆踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obon#Bon_Odori|1=Bon Odori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1152364}}
* {{ill|Parade|en|Parade|WD=Q657449|lt=Parades}}
* {{ill|Fireworks|ja|花火大会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fireworks|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 花火|1=firework|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q127933}}
* {{ill|Market stall|en|Market stall|WD=Q16556730|lt=Yatai}} (food stalls, often at {{ill|Ennichi|en|Ennichi|WD=Q985655|lt=Ennichi}})
* {{ill|Matsuri float|en|Matsuri float|WD=Q11470974|lt=Floats}} and {{ill|Festival music|ja|祭囃子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival music|qq=}}

[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Otheruses}}
{{Unreferenced|date=August 2014}}
{{nihongo|Natsumatsuri|夏祭り|1=Natsu Matsuri}} refers to the general term for {{ill|summer festivals|ja|夏祭り|1=Summer Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/summer festivals|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsu Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430090}} held in {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. These festivals are typically held from early July to late August, although festivals held in September that correspond to the {{ill|lunisolar calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lunisolar calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}'s {{ill|July (Lunisolar)|ja|7月 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/qq|lt=seventh month|qq=}} are often treated as summer festivals<ref>Examples include Kimata Town Fireworks Festival and Arima Town Bon Dance.</ref>.

== Overview ==
Many of Japan's summer festivals are rooted in events such as {{ill|Obon|ja|盂蘭盆会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obon|1=Ullambana Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15898039}} (盂蘭盆会), {{ill|Qixi Festival|en|Qixi Festival|WD=Q13423122|lt=Tanabata}}, and {{ill|Gion Matsuri|en|Gion Matsuri|WD=Q979873|lt=Gion Matsuri}}, or related rituals. As such, these festivals align with the sixth and seventh months of the old lunisolar calendar. In rural areas, these events often originated as part of relieving labor fatigue from summer farming activities. In urban areas, they are believed to stem from pre-{{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}} traditions of warding off summer epidemics and honoring the dead.

However, modernization has caused significant transformations in these festivals, and their origins are often not recognized. Summer festivals are now largely regarded as vibrant and festive events rather than solemn ones. Furthermore, many festivals lack origins before the Edo period, having been introduced in the modern era or modeled after traditional festivals from other regions.

Especially around the {{ill|Obon|ja|盂蘭盆会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obon|1=Ullambana Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15898039}} season in August, summer festivals are often associated with homecoming, creating opportunities for emotional reunions with loved ones{{Citation needed|date=October 2014}}.

In port towns, variations rich in local flavor, such as "Minato Matsuri" (harbor festivals), are common. Many {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} festivals also take place during this period.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Summer festivals in Japan]][[ja:夏祭り]][[zh:夏祭]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Summer festivals in Japan]][[ja:夏祭り]][[zh:夏祭]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Summer festivals in Japan]][[ja:夏祭り]][[zh:夏祭]]

{{Otheruses}}
{{Unreferenced|date=August 2014}}
{{nihongo|Natsumatsuri|夏祭り|1=Natsu Matsuri}} refers to the general term for {{ill|summer festivals|ja|夏祭り|1=Summer Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/summer festivals|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsu Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430090}} held in {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. These festivals are typically held from early July to late August, although festivals held in September that correspond to the {{ill|lunisolar calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lunisolar calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}'s {{ill|July (Lunisolar)|ja|7月 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/qq|lt=seventh month|qq=}} are often treated as summer festivals<ref>Examples include Kimata Town Fireworks Festival and Arima Town Bon Dance.</ref>.

== Overview ==
Many of Japan's summer festivals are rooted in events such as {{ill|Obon|ja|盂蘭盆会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obon|1=Ullambana Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15898039}} (盂蘭盆会), {{ill|Qixi Festival|en|Qixi Festival|WD=Q13423122|lt=Tanabata}}, and {{ill|Gion Matsuri|en|Gion Matsuri|WD=Q979873|lt=Gion Matsuri}}, or related rituals. As such, these festivals align with the sixth and seventh months of the old lunisolar calendar. In rural areas, these events often originated as part of relieving labor fatigue from summer farming activities. In urban areas, they are believed to stem from pre-{{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}} traditions of warding off summer epidemics and honoring the dead.

However, modernization has caused significant transformations in these festivals, and their origins are often not recognized. Summer festivals are now largely regarded as vibrant and festive events rather than solemn ones. Furthermore, many festivals lack origins before the Edo period, having been introduced in the modern era or modeled after traditional festivals from other regions.

Especially around the {{ill|Obon|ja|盂蘭盆会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obon|1=Ullambana Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15898039}} season in August, summer festivals are often associated with homecoming, creating opportunities for emotional reunions with loved ones{{Citation needed|date=October 2014}}.

In port towns, variations rich in local flavor, such as "Minato Matsuri" (harbor festivals), are common. Many {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} festivals also take place during this period.

== Events Held at Summer Festivals ==
* {{ill|Obon#Bon_Odori|lt=Bon Dance|ja|盆踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obon#Bon_Odori|1=Bon Odori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1152364}}
* {{ill|Parade|en|Parade|WD=Q657449|lt=Parades}}
* {{ill|Fireworks|ja|花火大会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fireworks|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 花火|1=firework|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q127933}}
* {{ill|Market stall|en|Market stall|WD=Q16556730|lt=Yatai}} (food stalls, often at {{ill|Ennichi|en|Ennichi|WD=Q985655|lt=Ennichi}})
* {{ill|Matsuri float|en|Matsuri float|WD=Q11470974|lt=Floats}} and {{ill|Festival music|ja|祭囃子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival music|qq=}}
* Events using temporary stages:
** Song shows
** Character shows (often featuring {{ill|Anime|ja|アニメ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anime|1=Anime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4066283}}, {{ill|Super Sentai|ja|戦隊シリーズ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Super Sentai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スーパー戦隊シリーズ|1=Super Sentai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1054760}}, or {{ill|Local hero (Japan)|en|Local hero (Japan)|WD=Q4259512|lt=Local hero (Japan)}})
** {{ill|Magic (illusion)|en|Magic (illusion)|WD=Q148442|lt=Magic (illusion)}} shows
** {{ill|Karaoke|en|Karaoke|WD=Q229345|lt=Karaoke}} competitions

{{nihongo|Natsumatsuri|夏祭り|1=Natsu Matsuri}} refers to the general term for {{ill|summer festivals|ja|夏祭り|1=Summer Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/summer festivals|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsu Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430090}} held in {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. These festivals are typically held from early July to late August, although festivals held in September that correspond to the {{ill|lunisolar calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lunisolar calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}'s {{ill|July (Lunisolar)|ja|7月 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/qq|lt=seventh month|qq=}} are often treated as summer festivals<ref>Examples include Kimata Town Fireworks Festival and Arima Town Bon Dance.</ref>.

== Overview ==
Many of Japan's summer festivals are rooted in events such as {{ill|Obon|ja|盂蘭盆会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obon|1=Ullambana Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15898039}} (盂蘭盆会), {{ill|Qixi Festival|en|Qixi Festival|WD=Q13423122|lt=Tanabata}}, and {{ill|Gion Matsuri|en|Gion Matsuri|WD=Q979873|lt=Gion Matsuri}}, or related rituals. As such, these festivals align with the sixth and seventh months of the old lunisolar calendar. In rural areas, these events often originated as part of relieving labor fatigue from summer farming activities. In urban areas, they are believed to stem from pre-{{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}} traditions of warding off summer epidemics and honoring the dead.

However, modernization has caused significant transformations in these festivals, and their origins are often not recognized. Summer festivals are now largely regarded as vibrant and festive events rather than solemn ones. Furthermore, many festivals lack origins before the Edo period, having been introduced in the modern era or modeled after traditional festivals from other regions.

Especially around the {{ill|Obon|ja|盂蘭盆会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obon|1=Ullambana Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15898039}} season in August, summer festivals are often associated with homecoming, creating opportunities for emotional reunions with loved ones{{Citation needed|date=October 2014}}.

In port towns, variations rich in local flavor, such as "Minato Matsuri" (harbor festivals), are common. Many {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} festivals also take place during this period.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Summer festivals in Japan]][[ja:夏祭り]][[zh:夏祭]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Summer festivals in Japan]][[ja:夏祭り]][[zh:夏祭]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Summer festivals in Japan]][[ja:夏祭り]][[zh:夏祭]]

{{Otheruses}}
{{Unreferenced|date=August 2014}}
{{nihongo|Natsumatsuri|夏祭り|1=Natsu Matsuri}} refers to the general term for {{ill|summer festivals|ja|夏祭り|1=Summer Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/summer festivals|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsu Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430090}} held in {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. These festivals are typically held from early July to late August, although festivals held in September that correspond to the {{ill|lunisolar calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lunisolar calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}'s {{ill|July (Lunisolar)|ja|7月 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/qq|lt=seventh month|qq=}} are often treated as summer festivals<ref>Examples include Kimata Town Fireworks Festival and Arima Town Bon Dance.</ref>.

== Overview ==
Many of Japan's summer festivals are rooted in events such as {{ill|Obon|ja|盂蘭盆会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obon|1=Ullambana Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15898039}} (盂蘭盆会), {{ill|Qixi Festival|en|Qixi Festival|WD=Q13423122|lt=Tanabata}}, and {{ill|Gion Matsuri|en|Gion Matsuri|WD=Q979873|lt=Gion Matsuri}}, or related rituals. As such, these festivals align with the sixth and seventh months of the old lunisolar calendar. In rural areas, these events often originated as part of relieving labor fatigue from summer farming activities. In urban areas, they are believed to stem from pre-{{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}} traditions of warding off summer epidemics and honoring the dead.

However, modernization has caused significant transformations in these festivals, and their origins are often not recognized. Summer festivals are now largely regarded as vibrant and festive events rather than solemn ones. Furthermore, many festivals lack origins before the Edo period, having been introduced in the modern era or modeled after traditional festivals from other regions.

Especially around the {{ill|Obon|ja|盂蘭盆会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obon|1=Ullambana Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15898039}} season in August, summer festivals are often associated with homecoming, creating opportunities for emotional reunions with loved ones{{Citation needed|date=October 2014}}.

In port towns, variations rich in local flavor, such as "Minato Matsuri" (harbor festivals), are common. Many {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} festivals also take place during this period.

== Events Held at Summer Festivals ==
* {{ill|Obon#Bon_Odori|lt=Bon Dance|ja|盆踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obon#Bon_Odori|1=Bon Odori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1152364}}
* {{ill|Parade|en|Parade|WD=Q657449|lt=Parades}}
* {{ill|Fireworks|ja|花火大会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fireworks|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 花火|1=firework|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q127933}}
* {{ill|Market stall|en|Market stall|WD=Q16556730|lt=Yatai}} (food stalls, often at {{ill|Ennichi|en|Ennichi|WD=Q985655|lt=Ennichi}})
* {{ill|Matsuri float|en|Matsuri float|WD=Q11470974|lt=Floats}} and {{ill|Festival music|ja|祭囃子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival music|qq=}}
* Events using temporary stages:
** Song shows
** Character shows (often featuring {{ill|Anime|ja|アニメ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anime|1=Anime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4066283}}, {{ill|Super Sentai|ja|戦隊シリーズ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Super Sentai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スーパー戦隊シリーズ|1=Super Sentai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1054760}}, or {{ill|Local hero (Japan)|en|Local hero (Japan)|WD=Q4259512|lt=Local hero (Japan)}})
** {{ill|Magic (illusion)|en|Magic (illusion)|WD=Q148442|lt=Magic (illusion)}} shows
** {{ill|Karaoke|en|Karaoke|WD=Q229345|lt=Karaoke}} competitions

In port towns, variations rich in local flavor, such as "Minato Matsuri" (harbor festivals), are common. Many {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} festivals also take place during this period.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Summer festivals in Japan]][[ja:夏祭り]][[zh:夏祭]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Summer festivals in Japan]][[ja:夏祭り]][[zh:夏祭]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Summer festivals in Japan]][[ja:夏祭り]][[zh:夏祭]]

{{Otheruses}}
{{Unreferenced|date=August 2014}}
{{nihongo|Natsumatsuri|夏祭り|1=Natsu Matsuri}} refers to the general term for {{ill|summer festivals|ja|夏祭り|1=Summer Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/summer festivals|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsu Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430090}} held in {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. These festivals are typically held from early July to late August, although festivals held in September that correspond to the {{ill|lunisolar calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lunisolar calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}'s {{ill|July (Lunisolar)|ja|7月 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/qq|lt=seventh month|qq=}} are often treated as summer festivals<ref>Examples include Kimata Town Fireworks Festival and Arima Town Bon Dance.</ref>.

== Overview ==
Many of Japan's summer festivals are rooted in events such as {{ill|Obon|ja|盂蘭盆会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obon|1=Ullambana Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15898039}} (盂蘭盆会), {{ill|Qixi Festival|en|Qixi Festival|WD=Q13423122|lt=Tanabata|1=Qixi Festival}}, and {{ill|Gion Matsuri|en|Gion Matsuri|WD=Q979873|lt=Gion Matsuri|1=Gion Matsuri}}, or related rituals. As such, these festivals align with the sixth and seventh months of the old lunisolar calendar. In rural areas, these events often originated as part of relieving labor fatigue from summer farming activities. In urban areas, they are believed to stem from pre-{{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}} traditions of warding off summer epidemics and honoring the dead.

However, modernization has caused significant transformations in these festivals, and their origins are often not recognized. Summer festivals are now largely regarded as vibrant and festive events rather than solemn ones. Furthermore, many festivals lack origins before the Edo period, having been introduced in the modern era or modeled after traditional festivals from other regions.

Especially around the {{ill|Obon|ja|盂蘭盆会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obon|1=Ullambana Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15898039}} season in August, summer festivals are often associated with homecoming, creating opportunities for emotional reunions with loved ones{{Citation needed|date=October 2014}}.

In port towns, variations rich in local flavor, such as "Minato Matsuri" (harbor festivals), are common. Many {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} festivals also take place during this period.

* {{ill|Matsuri float|en|Matsuri float|WD=Q11470974|lt=Floats|1=Matsuri float}} and {{ill|Festival music|ja|祭囃子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival music|qq=}}

** {{ill|Karaoke|en|Karaoke|WD=Q229345|lt=Karaoke|1=Karaoke}} competitions

{{Otheruses}}
{{Unreferenced|date=August 2014}}
{{nihongo|Natsumatsuri|夏祭り|1=Natsu Matsuri}} refers to the general term for {{ill|summer festivals|ja|夏祭り|1=Summer Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/summer festivals|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsu Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430090}} held in {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. These festivals are typically held from early July to late August, although festivals held in September that correspond to the {{ill|lunisolar calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lunisolar calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}'s {{ill|July (Lunisolar)|ja|7月 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/qq|lt=seventh month|qq=}} are often treated as summer festivals<ref>Examples include Kimata Town Fireworks Festival and Arima Town Bon Dance.</ref>.

== Overview ==
Many of Japan's summer festivals are rooted in events such as {{ill|Obon|ja|盂蘭盆会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obon|1=Ullambana Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15898039}} (盂蘭盆会), {{ill|Qixi Festival|en|Qixi Festival|WD=Q13423122|lt=Tanabata|1=Qixi Festival}}, and {{ill|Gion Matsuri|en|Gion Matsuri|WD=Q979873|lt=Gion Matsuri|1=Gion Matsuri}}, or related rituals. As such, these festivals align with the sixth and seventh months of the old lunisolar calendar. In rural areas, these events often originated as part of relieving labor fatigue from summer farming activities. In urban areas, they are believed to stem from pre-{{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}} traditions of warding off summer epidemics and honoring the dead.

However, modernization has caused significant transformations in these festivals, and their origins are often not recognized. Summer festivals are now largely regarded as vibrant and festive events rather than solemn ones. Furthermore, many festivals lack origins before the Edo period, having been introduced in the modern era or modeled after traditional festivals from other regions.

Especially around the {{ill|Obon|ja|盂蘭盆会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obon|1=Ullambana Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15898039}} season in August, summer festivals are often associated with homecoming, creating opportunities for emotional reunions with loved ones{{Citation needed|date=October 2014}}.

In port towns, variations rich in local flavor, such as "Minato Matsuri" (harbor festivals), are common. Many {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} festivals also take place during this period.

{{Otheruses}}
{{Unreferenced|date=August 2014}}
{{nihongo|Natsumatsuri|夏祭り|1=Natsu Matsuri}} refers to the general term for {{ill|summer festivals|ja|夏祭り|1=Summer Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/summer festivals|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsu Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430090}} held in {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. These festivals are typically held from early July to late August, although festivals held in September that correspond to the {{ill|lunisolar calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lunisolar calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}'s {{ill|July (Lunisolar)|ja|7月 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/qq|lt=seventh month|qq=}} are often treated as summer festivals<ref>Examples include Kimata Town Fireworks Festival and Arima Town Bon Dance.</ref>.

== Overview ==
Many of Japan's summer festivals are rooted in events such as {{ill|Obon|ja|盂蘭盆会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obon|1=Ullambana Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15898039}} (盂蘭盆会), {{ill|Qixi Festival|en|Qixi Festival|WD=Q13423122|lt=Tanabata|1=Qixi Festival}}, and {{ill|Gion Matsuri|en|Gion Matsuri|WD=Q979873|lt=Gion Matsuri|1=Gion Matsuri}}, or related rituals. As such, these festivals align with the sixth and seventh months of the old lunisolar calendar. In rural areas, these events often originated as part of relieving labor fatigue from summer farming activities. In urban areas, they are believed to stem from pre-{{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}} traditions of warding off summer epidemics and honoring the dead.

However, modernization has caused significant transformations in these festivals, and their origins are often not recognized. Summer festivals are now largely regarded as vibrant and festive events rather than solemn ones. Furthermore, many festivals lack origins before the Edo period, having been introduced in the modern era or modeled after traditional festivals from other regions.

Especially around the {{ill|Obon|ja|盂蘭盆会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obon|1=Ullambana Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15898039}} season in August, summer festivals are often associated with homecoming, creating opportunities for emotional reunions with loved ones{{Citation needed|date=October 2014}}.

In port towns, variations rich in local flavor, such as "Minato Matsuri" (harbor festivals), are common. Many {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} festivals also take place during this period.

revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24155476638|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Summer festivals in Japan]][[ja:夏祭り]][[zh:夏祭]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Summer festivals in Japan]][[ja:夏祭り]][[zh:夏祭]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:夏祭り]][[zh:夏祭]]

{{Otheruses}}
{{Unreferenced|date=August 2014}}
{{nihongo|Natsumatsuri|夏祭り|1=Natsu Matsuri}} refers to the general term for {{ill|summer festivals|ja|夏祭り|1=Summer Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/summer festivals|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsu Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430090}} held in {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. These festivals are typically held from early July to late August, although festivals held in September that correspond to the {{ill|lunisolar calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lunisolar calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}'s {{ill|July (Lunisolar)|ja|7月 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/qq|lt=seventh month|qq=}} are often treated as summer festivals<ref>Examples include Kimata Town Fireworks Festival and Arima Town Bon Dance.</ref>.

== Overview ==
Many of Japan's summer festivals are rooted in events such as {{ill|Obon|ja|盂蘭盆会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obon|1=Ullambana Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15898039}} (盂蘭盆会), {{ill|Qixi Festival|en|Qixi Festival|WD=Q13423122|lt=Tanabata|1=Qixi Festival}}, and {{ill|Gion Matsuri|en|Gion Matsuri|WD=Q979873|lt=Gion Matsuri|1=Gion Matsuri}}, or related rituals. As such, these festivals align with the sixth and seventh months of the old lunisolar calendar. In rural areas, these events often originated as part of relieving labor fatigue from summer farming activities. In urban areas, they are believed to stem from pre-{{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}} traditions of warding off summer epidemics and honoring the dead.

However, modernization has caused significant transformations in these festivals, and their origins are often not recognized. Summer festivals are now largely regarded as vibrant and festive events rather than solemn ones. Furthermore, many festivals lack origins before the Edo period, having been introduced in the modern era or modeled after traditional festivals from other regions.

Especially around the {{ill|Obon|ja|盂蘭盆会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obon|1=Ullambana Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15898039}} season in August, summer festivals are often associated with homecoming, creating opportunities for emotional reunions with loved ones{{Citation needed|date=October 2014}}.

In port towns, variations rich in local flavor, such as "Minato Matsuri" (harbor festivals), are common. Many {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} festivals also take place during this period.

== Events Held at Summer Festivals ==
* {{ill|Obon#Bon_Odori|lt=Bon Dance|ja|盆踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obon#Bon_Odori|1=Bon Odori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1152364}}
* {{ill|Parade|en|Parade|WD=Q657449|lt=Parades|1=Parade}}
* {{ill|Fireworks|ja|花火大会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fireworks|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 花火|1=Fireworks|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q127933}}
* {{ill|Market stall|en|Market stall|WD=Q16556730|lt=Yatai|1=Market stall}} (food stalls, often at {{ill|Ennichi|en|Ennichi|WD=Q985655|lt=Ennichi|1=Ennichi}})
* {{ill|Matsuri float|en|Matsuri float|WD=Q11470974|lt=Floats|1=Matsuri float}} and {{ill|Festival music|ja|祭囃子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival music|qq=}}
* Events using temporary stages:
** Song shows
** Character shows (often featuring {{ill|Anime|ja|アニメ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anime|1=Q4066283|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4066283}}, {{ill|Super Sentai|ja|戦隊シリーズ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Super Sentai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スーパー戦隊シリーズ|1=Super Sentai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1054760}}, or {{ill|Local hero (Japan)|en|Local hero (Japan)|WD=Q4259512|lt=Local hero (Japan)|1=Local hero (Japan)}})
** {{ill|Magic (illusion)|en|Magic (illusion)|WD=Q148442|lt=Magic (illusion)|1=Magic (illusion)}} shows
** {{ill|Karaoke|en|Karaoke|WD=Q229345|lt=Karaoke|1=Karaoke}} competitions
